title: Guazuma guazuma
taxonomy: Guazuma guazuma is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to the Americas, specifically in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant belongs to the genus Guazuma, which consists of several species of trees and shrubs. Guazuma guazuma is also known as the Guanacaste tree, and it is valued for its medicinal properties and timber. This species is widely distributed across Central and South America, and it has been naturalized in other parts of the world.
uses: Guazuma guazuma is a tree native to Central America and has been traditionally used in medicine. Its bark, leaves, and seeds have been used to treat various health conditions. In traditional medicine, Guazuma guazuma is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. Its medicinal uses are still being studied for their potential in modern medicine.

intro: Guazuma ulmifolia, commonly known as Guazuma guazuma, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been linked to the potential treatment of various health conditions.
